paper.title: A kinematic and structural study of young open clusters in the Milky Way Galaxy using Gaia DR3 catalogue

review.summary: Seven internal tables: GMM cluster proper-motion parameters (table:GMM), isochrone-fitting results from least-squares and MCMC (table_isofit), radial-velocity and space-velocity estimates (table_sp_vel), transverse-velocity dispersion by spectral type (table_obfdisp), spectral-type counts and M/K ratios (table_obf), literature cluster parameters from Cantat-Gaudin (table_apx), and comparison with previous studies (Table:Compare). Two external resources: the GaiaTools cluster-fitting code on GitHub and the CMD isochrone web interface.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the mean proper-motion components and membership counts for 14 open clusters derived from Gaussian mixture model (GMM) analysis of Gaia DR3 astrometry.

source_refs.caption: \textit{Cluster parameters obtained for each cluster using GMM. Columns 2-3 are the two components of PM along with their errors. $N_{star}$ represents the number of sources with probability $\ge$90$\%$}
source_refs.label: table:GMM

columns.name: pmra | (mas yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Mean proper motion in right ascension direction

columns.name: pmdec | (mas yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Mean proper motion in declination direction

columns.name: N_star
columns.meaning: Number of member stars with membership probability >= 90%

internal_tables.evidence: \textit{Cluster parameters obtained for each cluster using GMM. Columns 2-3 are the two components of PM along with their errors. $N_{star}$ represents the number of sources with probability $\ge$90$\%$}

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents isochrone-fitting results for 14 open clusters using both least-squares minimization and MCMC, yielding distance, age, metallicity, and extinction estimates.

source_refs.caption: \textit{Isochrone fitting results from least-square minimization and MCMC. D, $log_{10}(age)$, [M/H], and $A_G$ are the distance, $log_{10}(age)$, metallicity, and extinction in G mag, respectively, for least-square minimization and MCMC.}
source_refs.label: table_isofit

columns.name: MCMC | log_10 (age) | (dex)
columns.meaning: Logarithm of age from least-squares minimization

columns.name: A_G | (mag)
columns.meaning: Extinction in G-band from least-squares minimization

internal_tables.evidence: \textit{Isochrone fitting results from least-square minimization and MCMC. D, $log_{10}(age)$, [M/H], and $A_G$ are the distance, $log_{10}(age)$, metallicity, and extinction in G mag, respectively, for least-square minimization and MCMC.}

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarises radial-velocity and transverse-velocity statistics for 14 open clusters, and combines them into total space velocities using Gaia DR3 data.

source_refs.caption: \textit{Radial velocity and space velocity estimation for the clusters. $N_{RV}$ represents the number of stars in a cluster with radial velocity information available in the \textit{Gaia} DR3. Columns 3--6 represent the Radial velocity mean and its dispersion, standard error, and measurement error, respectively. $N_{star}$ is the number of cluster members. Columns 8--10 are the Transverse velocity mean and its dispersion and standard error, respectively. Columns 11 and 12 represent the space velocity and its error.}
source_refs.label: table_sp_vel

columns.name: N_RV
columns.meaning: Number of stars with radial velocity in Gaia DR3

columns.name: RV_std_er | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Standard error of the mean radial velocity

columns.name: Tvel_std_er | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Standard error of the mean transverse velocity

internal_tables.evidence: \textit{Radial velocity and space velocity estimation for the clusters. $N_{RV}$ represents the number of stars in a cluster with radial velocity information available in the \textit{Gaia} DR3. Columns 3--6 represent the Radial velocity mean and its dispersion, standard error, and measurement error, respectively. $N_{star}$ is the number of cluster members. Columns 8--10 are the Transverse velocity mean and its dispersion and standard error, respectively. Columns 11 and 12 represent the space velocity and its error.}

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports the mean mass and transverse-velocity dispersion for four coarse spectral-type bins (BA, FG, K, M) in each cluster, used to assess dynamical mass segregation.

source_refs.caption: \textit{Transverse velocity and its dispersion for each spectral type. Columns 2--5 represent the mean mass of each spectral type, and columns 6--9 represent the dispersion in transverse velocity along with their errors for the mentioned group of spectral types.}
source_refs.label: table_obfdisp

columns.name: BA_Tvel_disp | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Transverse velocity dispersion of BA-type stars

columns.name: FG_Tvel_disp | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Transverse velocity dispersion of FG-type stars

columns.name: K_Tvel_disp | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Transverse velocity dispersion of K-type stars

columns.name: M_Tvel_disp | (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Transverse velocity dispersion of M-type stars

internal_tables.evidence: \textit{Transverse velocity and its dispersion for each spectral type. Columns 2--5 represent the mean mass of each spectral type, and columns 6--9 represent the dispersion in transverse velocity along with their errors for the mentioned group of spectral types.}

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Counts the number of cluster members in each spectral-type bin (B, A, F, G, K, M) and reports the observed and theoretical M/K star ratios for 14 open clusters.

source_refs.caption: \textit{Spectral type classification of cluster members. Columns 2--7 represent the number of sources belonging to each spectral type for the corresponding clusters mentioned in column 1. Columns 8 and 9 represent the mass range of the cluster, with Mass\_min representing minimum mass and Mass\_max representing maximum mass in solar mass ($M_\odot$). Column 10 represents the observed ratio of M-type stars to the K-type stars in corresponding clusters, which are the lower bound values. Columns 11 and 12 represent expected M/K ratios from Salpeter \citep{Salpeter1955} and Kroupa \citep{Kroupa2001}, respectively. }
source_refs.label: table_obf

columns.name: Mass_min | (M_\odot)
columns.meaning: Minimum stellar mass in the cluster

columns.name: Mass_max | (M_\odot)
columns.meaning: Maximum stellar mass in the cluster

columns.name: M/K
columns.meaning: Observed lower-bound ratio of M-type to K-type stars

columns.name: M/K Salpeter
columns.meaning: Expected M/K ratio from a Salpeter initial mass function

columns.name: M/K Kroupa
columns.meaning: Expected M/K ratio from a Kroupa initial mass function

internal_tables.evidence: \textit{Spectral type classification of cluster members. Columns 2--7 represent the number of sources belonging to each spectral type for the corresponding clusters mentioned in column 1. Columns 8 and 9 represent the mass range of the cluster, with Mass\_min representing minimum mass and Mass\_max representing maximum mass in solar mass ($M_\odot$). Column 10 represents the observed ratio of M-type stars to the K-type stars in corresponding clusters, which are the lower bound values. Columns 11 and 12 represent expected M/K ratios from Salpeter \citep{Salpeter1955} and Kroupa \citep{Kroupa2001}, respectively. }

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Appendix table compiling literature cluster parameters from the Cantat-Gaudin catalogue for comparison with the new measurements.

source_refs.caption: \textit{Cluster parameters obtained from literature survey \cite{CG}. Column 2 and 3 represent the coordinates of the corresponding cluster, RA and Dec, respectively. Columns 4 and 5 represent the PM along RA and Dec, respectively. D is the distance to the cluster, r50 is the radius containing half of the cluster members, Rmax is the r50$\times$2 in arcminutes, and the last column represents the $log_{10}(age)$.}
source_refs.label: table_apx

columns.name: pmra^* | (mas yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension from Cantat-Gaudin catalogue

columns.name: pmdec^* | (mas yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination from Cantat-Gaudin catalogue

columns.name: r50^* | (deg)
columns.meaning: Radius containing half of the cluster members

columns.name: Rmax | (arcmin)
columns.meaning: r50 multiplied by 2, expressed in arcminutes

columns.name: r50\times2 | (deg)
columns.meaning: r50 multiplied by 2, expressed in degrees

columns.name: log_10(age)^*
columns.meaning: Logarithm of age from Cantat-Gaudin catalogue

internal_tables.evidence: \textit{Cluster parameters obtained from literature survey \cite{CG}. Column 2 and 3 represent the coordinates of the corresponding cluster, RA and Dec, respectively. Columns 4 and 5 represent the PM along RA and Dec, respectively. D is the distance to the cluster, r50 is the radius containing half of the cluster members, Rmax is the r50$\times$2 in arcminutes, and the last column represents the $log_{10}(age)$.}

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Compares the number of members, distances, and ages derived in this work with values from Cantat-Gaudin, Kharchenko, and Dias catalogues for 14 open clusters.

source_refs.caption: \textit{Comparison of cluster parameters with previous studies such as \cite{CG, Kharchenko2016, Dias2021}}
source_refs.label: Table:Compare

columns.name: \multicolumn3c|N_star | This study
columns.meaning: Number of member stars from this study

columns.name: D(pc) | Cantat -Gaudin
columns.meaning: Number of member stars from Cantat-Gaudin catalogue

columns.name: \multicolumn4clog_10(age) (dex) | Dias
columns.meaning: Number of member stars from Dias catalogue

columns.name: This study
columns.meaning: Logarithm of age from this study

columns.name: Cantat-Gaudin
columns.meaning: Logarithm of age from Cantat-Gaudin catalogue

columns.name: Kharchenko
columns.meaning: Logarithm of age from Kharchenko catalogue

columns.name: Dias
columns.meaning: Logarithm of age from Dias catalogue

internal_tables.evidence: \textit{Comparison of cluster parameters with previous studies such as \cite{CG, Kharchenko2016, Dias2021}}

external_resources.description: GaiaTools cluster-fitting code (run_fit.py) used in this work for Gaussian mixture model membership analysis and isochrone fitting.
CMD stellar evolution tracks and isochrone web interface used to derive cluster distances, ages, metallicities, and extinctions.
